title: You are an Architect reviewing a VOICEVOX PNG portrait + lip-sync implementation for a Remotion video generation system. ## TASK Review the architecture of the PNG portrait system addition. Evaluate separation of concerns, extensibility, and integration quality. ## CONTEXT 6 files changed (2 new, 4...
summary_text: { "score": 1, "findings": [ { "severity": "critical", "description": "PNG mode is activated from speaker metadata alone and does not verify that `public/characters/.../portrait.png` actually exists. At the same time, `generate-video.ts` now always emits `speakerId` (default `1`), so a machine that has not run portrait setup can hit missing-image behavior (or render failure) instead of gracefully using SVG fallback.", "suggestion": "Make PNG mode conditional on both metadata and confirmed asset presence. Add a preflight check in the render pipeline (or a generated manifest) and force SVG fallback when portrait files are missing. If portraits are required for known speakers, fail fast with a clear setup error." }, { "severity": "major", "description": "Integration is inconsistent across render entry points: `generate-video.ts` propagates `speakerId` and `moraTimings`, but `render-enhanced.ts` still writes props without `speakerId` and updates scenes without `moraTimings`. This creates divergent behavior depending on which pipeline is used.", "suggestion": "Unify both scripts behind one shared render-props builder/update function so `speakerId` and `moraTimings` are always propagated identically." }, { "severity": "major", "description": "SSOT is only partial. Character identity data is centralized in `voicevox-characters.ts`, but portrait download targets are duplicated in `setup-voicevox-portraits.ts` as a separate list. Adding/updating a character now requires edits in multiple files, which will drift over time.", "suggestion": "Promote a single source model that includes download metadata (repo path or selector) and consume it from both runtime lookup and setup script." }, { "severity": "minor", "description": "`CharacterPortrait` uses CSS transitions (`transition: transform/filter ...`) for speaking animation. This is time-based browser animation rather than purely frame-driven animation, which is weaker for deterministic Remotion rendering guarantees.", "suggestion": "Replace CSS transitions with frame-derived interpolation/spring values only, so each frame is fully determined by `frame` + props." } ], "summary": "The separation between metadata (`voicevox-characters`), rendering (`CharacterLayer`/`character-parts`), and pipeline wiring is directionally good and the optional `speakerId` contract preserves API-level backward compatibility. However, the current integration has production-risk gaps (asset presence handling, dual-pipeline inconsistency, and metadata duplication), so this is not yet an extensible/robust architecture." }
